frameworks.name: cortical topographic maps
frameworks.one_line: Continuous cortical representations where preferred feature varies gradually across surface

frameworks.name: population receptive field (pRF) modeling
frameworks.one_line: Voxel-level tuning estimation applied to non-spatial quantity dimensions

frameworks.name: numerosity tuning
frameworks.one_line: Prior parietal numerosity map extended to a second quantity dimension

core_question: Does human parietal cortex contain a topographic map of object size that overlaps with the numerosity map?
key_assumptions: Quantity dimensions can be represented as topographic maps analogous to sensory cortices
pRF modeling generalizes from visual position to non-spatial features
Object size and numerosity can be decorrelated from visual field position experimentally
manipulations: Used stimuli minimizing correlations between object size and retinal position
Compared object-size and numerosity tuning in the same parietal region
key_findings: Object size preferences are organized as topographic maps in parietal cortex
Size-selective responses overlap the previously identified numerosity map
Common organizational properties carry over: more cortex and finer tuning for small magnitudes
interpretations: Parietal cortex hosts overlapping topographic maps for distinct quantity dimensions
Topographic organization may be a general principle for quantity processing, not only sensory inputs
